title: Object endpoint trusts client-supplied tenant ID — cross-tenant read
summary: A read endpoint resolves the object by ID without scoping the query to the caller's tenant, allowing a user in one tenant to read another tenant's object. SYNTHETIC EXAMPLE — not a real finding.
invalidators: A tenant-scoping filter is applied in the repository layer (not just the controller)
An authorization interceptor rejects cross-tenant object IDs before the handler runs

evidence.live_evidence: (empty — candidate is code-only until reproduced with two authenticated identities)
evidence.repro_script: two-jar tenant_diff: request the same object ID as tenant A and tenant B; a leak shows identical bodies
